It's a persimmon tree that overhangs the sidewalk. If you get to the fruit that fell the night before before the school kids kick them and stomp them, they can be very good, but they can also be astringent if not ripe enough. Access: Private but overhanging. F
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

Plainly visible from the road, not on privately owned land, but far enough into the knee high growth that you probably want to check yourself for ticks after browsing. The plant looks large and vigorous. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-September.
